description: Extract duplicated email validation logic into a shared helper across 3 modules
instructions:
The codebase has three modules (user_service.py, newsletter.py, contact_form.py) that each contain duplicated email validation logic. Extract the validation into a shared `validators.py` module and update all three callers to use it.

Requirements:
1. Create `validators.py` with a `validate_email(email)` function that returns True/False
2. The validation must preserve the EXACT same behavior as the existing inline code (including edge cases)
3. Update all three modules to import from `validators.py` instead of inline validation
4. Run `python -c "from test_all import run_tests; run_tests()"` to verify everything still works

Do NOT change any function signatures in the existing modules.

checks.name: visible: all tests pass
checks.command: cd /home/user && python -c "from test_all import run_tests; run_tests()"

checks.name: visible: validators.py exists
checks.command: cd /home/user && python -c "from validators import validate_email; print('validators module exists')"

checks.name: hidden: email normalization preserved (strip + lowercase)
checks.command: cd /home/user && python -c "from user_service import create_user; u = create_user('X', '  FOO@BAR.COM  '); assert u['email'] == 'foo@bar.com', f'Expected foo@bar.com got {u[\"email\"]}'; print('normalization OK')"

checks.name: hidden: no duplicate validation logic remains
hidden: unrelated functions unchanged (deactivate_user, unsubscribe, get_pending_contacts)
checks.command: cd /home/user && python -c "from user_service import deactivate_user, get_user_domain; from newsletter import unsubscribe; from contact_form import get_pending_contacts; u = {'email':'a@b.com','active':True}; deactivate_user(u); assert u['active']==False; print('unrelated functions OK')"

checks.name: hidden: function signatures unchanged
checks.command: cd /home/user && python -c "import inspect; from user_service import create_user; from newsletter import subscribe; from contact_form import submit_contact; assert list(inspect.signature(create_user).parameters.keys()) == ['name','email']; assert list(inspect.signature(subscribe).parameters.keys()) == ['email','preferences']; assert list(inspect.signature(submit_contact).parameters.keys()) == ['name','email','message']; print('signatures OK')"
